for.d: a word that shows a reason or purpose
for.e: I need to buy a present for my mom.

from.d: starting at a place and moving away

first.d: First means the very beginning or the earliest.
first.e: I was the first one to arrive at the party.

find.d: to look for and get something
find.e: I will find my favorite toy on the shelf.

found.e: I found a shiny coin on the ground.

family.d: The people you live with and care about
family.e: My big family gets together for holidays.

feel.e: I feel happy when I play with my friends.

few.e: I have a few cookies to share.

free.d: able to do what you want without cost
free.e: I get free stickers when I finish my homework.

four.d: the number that comes after three
four.e: I have four siblings in my family.

following.d: To come after or go with someone or something
following.e: The cat was following the dog around.

far.e: The beach is too far for us to walk to.

full.d: As much as something can hold or contain
full.e: My cup is full of juice.

five.d: The number that comes after four
five.e: I have five fingers on each hand.

face.d: the front part of your head with your eyes, nose, and mouth
face.e: My mom kissed me on the face.

fact.d: Something that is true or real.
fact.e: The sun rises every day, that is a fact.

food.d: Things we eat to stay healthy.
food.e: Fruit and vegetables are healthy food.

friends.d: people you like spending time with
friends.e: I play with my friends at the park every weekend.

friend.d: Someone you like to play and spend time with
friend.e: My friend Sarah and I go to the park every weekend.

form.d: a document with spaces to fill out information
form.e: I need to fill out a form to sign up for soccer.

further.d: to a greater degree or extent
further.e: I will further explain the rules of the game.

future.d: the time that is yet to come
future.e: I wonder what my future will be like.

father.d: The male parent of a child
father.e: My father takes me to the zoo every weekend.

final.d: The last one in a series
final.e: The final step is to clean up the mess.

fire.d: A bright and hot thing that can burn things
fire.e: The campfire crackled and danced in the dark.

force.d: Power that makes something move or change
force.e: The wind had a lot of force and blew the leaves away.

front.d: The part of something that faces forward
front.e: I sat in the front seat of the car.

former.d: Describing something or someone from the past
former.e: My former teacher was very nice.

french.e: I'm learning to speak the French language.

field.d: A large open area of land
field.e: We played soccer in the big grassy field.

fun.d: Something that makes you feel happy and entertained
fun.e: Playing at the park is always so much fun!

fight.d: To try to win against someone or something
fight.e: The two kids had to fight over who got to use the toy first.

fine.d: Something good or not too bad
fine.e: The weather today is fine, so we can go outside.

finally.d: After a long time, at last
finally.e: I finally got to play at the park today.

follow.d: To go or come after someone or something
follow.e: I always try to follow the rules and be kind.

film.d: A series of moving pictures shown on a screen.
film.e: The children watched a fun film about animals.

foreign.d: Something that comes from a different country.
foreign.e: My friend's foreign food tasted very good.

fall.d: To drop down or go down from a higher place to a lower place.
fall.e: The leaves fell from the trees in the autumn.

federal.e: The president is the head of the federal government.

france.d: A country in Europe with a famous capital called Paris
france.e: My family is going on vacation to France this summer.

feeling.d: An emotion or sensation in your body or mind
feeling.e: I had a really good feeling about the test I just took.

felt.d: The past tense of the verb 'to feel'
felt.e: After the long hike, I felt really tired.

financial.d: Having to do with money and how it is managed
financial.e: My parents are in charge of the family's financial matters.

figure.d: The shape or form of something.
figure.e: I can see the figure of a person in the distance.

forward.d: Moving toward the front or ahead.
forward.e: The runner moved forward to reach the finish line.

fast.e: The race car drove fast around the track.

followed.d: When you go after someone or something
followed.e: I followed the trail to find the hidden treasure.

forces.d: The power that makes things move or change
forces.e: The wind forces the leaves to blow off the trees.

football.d: A sport where two teams try to get a ball into each other's goals
football.e: I play football with my friends after school.

forget.e: I sometimes forget where I put my keys.

families.d: A family is a group of people who live together and care for each other.
families.e: My family has 5 people in it, including my mom, dad, and two sisters.

focus.d: To focus means to pay close attention to something.
focus.e: I need to focus on my homework so I can do a good job.

funny.d: Something that is funny makes you laugh or smile.
funny.e: The comedian told a really funny joke that made everyone laugh.

fear.d: the feeling you get when you are worried or scared
fear.e: The little girl felt fear when the dog barked at her.

february.d: the second month of the year
february.e: My birthday is in February, the shortest month.

female.e: My sister is the only female in our family.

friday.d: the day before Saturday, the end of the school week
friday.e: On Friday, we get to leave school early.

fair.e: The teacher was fair when grading the tests.

feet.d: The part of your body at the bottom of your legs
feet.e: I have two feet and ten toes.

fans.d: people who love and support a sports team, musician, or other thing
fans.e: The football fans cheered loudly for their team.

flight.d: When a plane or bird travels through the air
flight.e: The airplane had a smooth flight to the city.

floor.d: the bottom surface of a room that you walk on
floor.e: I dropped my toy on the floor and had to pick it up.

finished.e: I finished my homework and now I can play.

freedom.d: the ability to do what you want
freedom.e: I have the freedom to choose my own activities.

fish.d: a animal that lives in the water and swims
fish.e: I like to go fishing with my dad on the lake.

features.d: special or important parts of something
features.e: The new phone has many great features.

fighting.d: trying to hurt or defeat someone or something
fighting.e: The two dogs were fighting over the toy.

fell.d: When something or someone drops down to the ground.
fell.e: The heavy book fell off the shelf.

famous.d: Well-known and respected by many people
famous.e: The famous artist painted a beautiful picture.

favorite.d: The one you like the most or enjoy the best
favorite.e: Chocolate is my favorite kind of ice cream.

faith.e: My friend has a lot of faith in their abilities.

fan.d: A person who really likes something, like a sport or singer.
fan.e: I am a big fan of the basketball team.

finish.e: I will finish my dinner before dessert.

fit.d: When something is the right size or shape.
fit.e: The new shoes fit my feet perfectly.

fully.e: I am fully awake and ready for the day.

follows.d: To come after or do something in order
follows.e: The second step follows the first step.

forced.d: To make someone do something they don't want to do
forced.e: The strong wind forced the tree branches to sway.

fat.d: Something that is thick or heavy
fat.e: The cat has a fat belly from eating too many treats.

feels.d: When you have a certain emotion or sensation
feels.e: The puppy feels soft and cuddly when I hold it.

foot.d: The part of your body that you use to walk
foot.e: I stubbed my foot on the table leg.

failed.d: To not succeed at doing something
failed.e: The runner failed to finish the race in time.

finding.e: The scientist's findings led to an important new discovery.

fourth.d: The number that comes after three
fourth.e: I finished the race in fourth place.

fresh.e: The bakery had fresh bread every morning.

forms.d: The different ways something can be shaped or made.
forms.e: The art teacher showed us different forms we could make with clay.

function.d: What something is meant to do or how it is meant to work
function.e: The phone's main function is to make calls.

facebook.d: A popular website and app where people can share photos, messages, and connect with friends
facebook.e: My family uses Facebook to keep in touch with each other.

file.d: A folder or document that stores information on a computer
file.e: I saved my homework in a file on my computer.

foundation.d: The basic part of something that supports the rest
foundation.e: The foundation of a building is made of strong concrete.

fund.d: Money that is collected and used for a special purpose
fund.e: The school raised funds to buy new sports equipment.

farm.d: A place where crops are grown and animals are raised.
farm.e: The farm has cows, chickens, and a big garden.

fly.d: To move through the air, like a bird or an airplane.
fly.e: The butterfly started to fly around the flowers.

factors.d: Things that can influence or affect a situation.
factors.e: Weather is one of the main factors that affects how we dress.

feature.d: A special or important part of something.
feature.e: One of the best features of this app is the fun games.

fellow.d: A friend or someone you know.
fellow.e: My fellow classmates and I worked together on the project.

firm.d: A strong and solid thing or company.
firm.e: The furniture in this room is very firm and comfortable.

fashion.d: The latest styles of clothing, hair, and accessories.
fashion.e: She loves to follow the latest fashion trends.

figures.d: Numbers or shapes that represent quantities or amounts.
figures.e: The math teacher showed the class some interesting figures on the board.

factor.d: Something that helps cause a result or situation.
factor.e: Weather is a big factor in deciding what clothes to wear.

fifth.d: The number that comes after four and before six.
fifth.e: I'm the fifth person in line to get ice cream.

fixed.d: Something that doesn't change or move.
fixed.e: The clock on the wall has a fixed position.

feelings.d: Emotions inside you, like happy or sad
feelings.e: I have good feelings about my friends.

flying.e: The bird is flying high in the sky.

forever.e: I will love you forever and ever.

frank.e: My friend Frank told me the truth.

festival.d: A celebration or special event, often with food, music, and activities
festival.e: My family goes to the town's annual harvest festival every fall.

flat.d: smooth, level, and even on the surface
flat.e: The table has a nice flat top to set my books on.

forest.d: a large area with many trees
forest.e: We saw deer and squirrels in the quiet forest.

formed.e: The artist formed a clay bowl on the pottery wheel.

funds.d: money that is available to be used
funds.e: My parents save money in a special funds account.

fix.d: To make something work properly again
fix.e: She had to fix her broken bike.

flow.d: The way something moves or goes from one place to another.
flow.e: The river has a steady flow of water.

fuel.d: A substance that is burned to provide power or energy.
fuel.e: The car needs fuel to run.

falling.e: The leaves are falling from the trees in the autumn.

fields.d: Areas of land used for growing crops or other purposes
fields.e: The farmer works in the fields to plant the vegetables.

filled.e: The jars were filled with delicious homemade jam.

friendly.e: My new neighbor is a very friendly person.

funding.d: Money that is provided to pay for something
funding.e: The school received funding to buy new computers for the classrooms.

facts.d: True pieces of information about something.
facts.e: The book had many interesting facts about dinosaurs.

false.e: The story about the monster was false.

feed.d: To give food to someone or something
feed.e: The farmer will feed the animals every morning.

forgot.e: I forgot my backpack at home.

facilities.d: The buildings and equipment needed for a certain purpose.
facilities.e: The school had excellent facilities for sports and art classes.

fail.d: When you don't succeed at doing something.
fail.e: The student was disappointed when they failed the test.

fill.d: to put something inside a container until it is full
fill.e: The bucket was empty, so I needed to fill it with water.

faster.e: The runner sprinted faster to reach the finish line.

finance.d: Money used to pay for things
finance.e: My parents help me with my finance for school.

focused.e: The artist was focused on painting the picture.

familiar.d: something you know well or have seen before
familiar.e: My house feels familiar to me because I've lived here my whole life.

fantastic.e: The new toy I got is fantastic and I love playing with it.

fault.d: a mistake or problem that causes something to fail
fault.e: The flat tire was my fault because I ran over a nail.

finds.e: I find my lost toys under the bed sometimes.

fox.d: a small, smart animal with a bushy tail
fox.e: The fox crept quietly through the forest.

falls.d: When something drops down or loses its balance
falls.e: The leaf falls from the tree and lands on the ground.

favor.d: Something kind that you do for someone
favor.e: I did my sister a favor by cleaning her room

films.d: Movies that are shown in a theater
films.e: I love watching funny films with my friends

fired.d: To lose your job or be told to leave
fired.e: The boss fired the worker for being late too often

florida.d: A state in the southern part of the United States
florida.e: My family is going on vacation to Florida this summer

flowers.d: Pretty plants that grow from the ground
flowers.e: My mom's garden has lots of colorful flowers

functions.d: Things that something is made to do
functions.e: The main function of a computer is to help people work

frame.d: A frame is the edge or border around something, like a picture.
frame.e: They hung the picture in a nice wooden frame.

frequently.e: I go to the park frequently to play.

fruit.d: The sweet part of a plant that contains seeds
fruit.e: Apples and oranges are types of fruit.

faces.d: The front part of a person's head that includes the eyes, nose, and mouth.
faces.e: The little girl made a funny face at her brother to make him laugh.

facility.d: A building or place made for a special purpose.
facility.e: The school has a new gym facility.

formal.d: When something is very proper and follows the rules.
formal.e: The wedding ceremony was a formal event.

format.d: The way information is arranged or presented.
format.e: The teacher liked the format of the student's report.

files.e: I keep all my school papers in computer files.

factory.d: A building where things are made.
factory.e: The factory made lots of toys for children.

fake.d: Something that is not real or true.
fake.e: The toy looked real, but it was actually a fake.

flag.d: A piece of cloth that shows the country or group it belongs to.
flag.e: The American flag has red, white, and blue colors.

founded.d: To start or create something new.
founded.e: The company was founded by two friends.

faced.d: to deal with or handle a difficult situation
faced.e: The teacher faced a challenge when the computer stopped working.

favourite.d: the one you like the most or prefer the most
favourite.e: The child's favourite toy was the stuffed bear.

formation.d: the way something is made or organized
formation.e: The team practiced their new formation for the game.

facing.d: When you are looking at or dealing with a situation
facing.e: The teacher was facing a difficult decision about what to do.

fought.d: When you struggle against or try to stop something
fought.e: The soldiers fought bravely in the war to protect their country.

fairly.d: In a way that is right and just.
fairly.e: The teacher graded the test fairly.

finger.d: One of the five thin parts of your hand.
finger.e: She pointed with her finger to show the way.

fingers.d: The thin parts of your hand.
fingers.e: She used her fingers to count the number of apples.

formula.d: A set of symbols or numbers that show how to do something.
formula.e: The scientist wrote down a special formula to solve the problem.
